loreRevision.designRule: Fiction feeders should be canon-derived; major systems stay mechanics-first.

nodes.title: Shattered Frontispiece
nodes.surface: A torn dedication plate fills the landing page; 12 shards can be dragged, rotated, and snapped together, each carrying one motif from a later region.
nodes.mechanics: Match edge tears, paper grain, and the repeated compass border; when assembled, the illuminated capitals on the repaired text spell ARCHIVE OF WAYS and expose the first internal route.
nodes.validation: Complete when the shard-state hash equals the canonical arrangement; auto-award Archive Address and reveal the /nexus route without a text entry box.
nodes.build_notes: Use a reusable drag/snap jigsaw component with keyboard rotation support and a "lock placed piece" affordance.
nodes.hint_1: The edge tears matter more than the words.
nodes.hint_2: The compass border forms one continuous ring when correct.
nodes.hint_3: Read only the capitals that glow after the final snap.
nodes.cross_talk: Establishes clear boundaries, gives an early win, and teaches that artifacts on the page matter.
nodes.step_detail: The landing page begins as a torn dedication plate. Reassemble the fragments to recover the archive address and the first unambiguous proof that the site is a designed game space rather than random ephemera.

nodes.title: Compass of Genres
nodes.surface: A brass compass shows twelve draggable icons around two rings: the outer ring holds eight fiction realms, the inner ring holds four math overlays.
nodes.mechanics: Sort each icon into its home sector, then rotate the rings until the icon notches line up with engraved tick marks; reading the engaged ticks clockwise yields the Bearings code.
nodes.validation: Validation compares icon-slot mapping plus wheel rotation; once correct, the page emits the Nexus Bearings token and lights eight dormant region sockets.
nodes.build_notes: Store icon definitions as JSON so later reskins are content-only.
nodes.hint_1: First place every icon; the rotation comes after sorting.
nodes.hint_2: Each sector has a flavor clue in its tooltip, so no slot is pure trivia.
nodes.hint_3: The winning extraction is the clockwise tick order, not the sector names.
nodes.cross_talk: Introduces the region structure and lets book/math knowledge feel rewarding without becoming trivia-gating.
nodes.step_detail: Sort twelve icons into eight fiction realms and four mathematical overlays, then rotate the compass wheel until the labels and symbols align.

nodes.title: The Marginal Index
nodes.surface: The front page is also an annotated faux title page: hover notes, alt-text, footnote numbers, and small margin marks can all be inspected.
nodes.mechanics: Find the twelve marked marginalia items and order them by their footnote number; the first letters form INDEXSTRING, which is later used as an ordering key.
nodes.validation: Complete when all twelve hidden marks have been found and arranged; accept either the ordered click trail or the normalized answer INDEXSTRING.
nodes.build_notes: Mark found annotations in save-state so revisits feel cumulative rather than punishing.
nodes.hint_1: The solution is hidden in the page furniture, not the body text.
nodes.hint_2: Every valid clue source has a tiny index mark next to it.
nodes.hint_3: Order the hits by footnote number before extracting letters.
nodes.cross_talk: Teaches the solver that the website itself is a puzzle surface and rewards close reading.
nodes.step_detail: Hover text, tooltips, sidenotes, and tiny marginal marks around the landing page hide an index string when read in the right order.

nodes.title: Observatory Calibration
nodes.surface: The observatory page shows a star field, a transparent sigil wheel, and a rotatable horizon ring unlocked by HUB01.
nodes.mechanics: Rotate the sky and the sigil wheel until four realm-constellations sit exactly on their matching glyphs; the sequence in which the rings illuminate gives the Constellation Order.
nodes.validation: Validation is exact overlay alignment within tolerance; the game saves the final angle pair and awards Constellation Order.
nodes.build_notes: Treat this as a generic two-layer rotation puzzle template reused later for cosmere overlays.
nodes.hint_1: The answer comes from matched overlays, not from naming stars.
nodes.hint_2: Only four constellations matter; the rest are visual noise.
nodes.hint_3: Record the order in which the rings light, not the final angle numbers.
nodes.cross_talk: Introduces layered visual puzzles and gives a satisfying toy-like interaction early.
nodes.step_detail: Rotate the observatory sky until constellations overlap the sigils of the realms; each correct overlap lights one ring of the nexus.

nodes.title: The First Crossroad
nodes.surface: A crossroad desk appears with four dials labeled Address, Bearings, Index, and Constellations, plus the repaired frontispiece as the extraction surface.
nodes.mechanics: Map each starter output into the desk: Address chooses a line, Bearings a compass direction, Index a character order, and Constellations a set of coordinates; pulling those letters yields the Wave-I Passkey.
nodes.validation: Validation accepts the normalized extracted string or the fully solved dial-state; success unlocks the first-wave region hubs.
nodes.build_notes: Make this a data-driven meta: each input names its extraction role rather than hardcoding letters in component code.
nodes.hint_1: Each of the four startup answers is used in a different way.
nodes.hint_2: Try asking what kind of selector each answer is: line, direction, order, coordinate.
nodes.hint_3: Overlay the four selectors on the dedication text, then read the chosen letters in index order.
nodes.cross_talk: This is the first confirmation-heavy chained puzzle: everything snaps cleanly into place and opens a wider frontier.
nodes.step_detail: Use the four startup answers as coordinates into the frontispiece to reveal the first passkey, unlocking the first wave of regions and the initial math overlays.

nodes.title: Path Seeding
nodes.surface: Path Seeding is a canon roster reconstruction page built from cradle sacred-arts basics, aura, madra, and iconic paths.
nodes.mechanics: Use canon knowledge of path identity, aura versus madra, signature Path behavior to match sacred artists to aura signatures and perform the solved cycle; then extract from resolved cycle order.
nodes.validation: Require the correct canonical mapping or state plus extraction from resolved cycle order.
nodes.build_notes: Use a reusable roster/dossier component with data-driven candidates and validation.
nodes.hint_1: Start with the most distinctive candidates or anchors.
nodes.hint_2: Lock identities before worrying about ordering or extraction.
nodes.hint_3: Do not extract until the full canonical mapping is stable.
nodes.cross_talk: Turns Cradle knowledge into the actual solve material rather than leaving the page as a themed wrapper.
nodes.step_detail: Path Seeding now asks the solver to match sacred artists to aura signatures and perform the solved cycle. Candidate set: Lindon, Yerin, Eithan, Mercy, Orthos, Ziel.
nodes.canon_scope: Cradle sacred-arts basics, aura, madra, and iconic Paths
nodes.fairness_scaffold: Visible candidate list, partial anchor facts, and schema-constrained cells prevent open-ended guessing.

nodes.title: Aura Atlas of Cradle
nodes.surface: Aura Atlas of Cradle is a canon geography / route reconstruction page built from major cradle locations and the aura signatures associated with them.
nodes.mechanics: Use canon knowledge of location-to-aura association, scene memory, route implications to map aura traces to locations and follow the solved site chain; then extract from solved site chain.
nodes.validation: Require the correct canonical mapping or state plus extraction from solved site chain.
nodes.build_notes: Reuse the route or atlas component; all geography and extraction logic should come from content data.
nodes.hint_1: Identify destinations or zones before optimizing any path.
nodes.hint_2: Map identity comes before route legality.
nodes.hint_3: Extract only from the solved route or waypoint chain.
nodes.cross_talk: Turns Cradle knowledge into the actual solve material rather than leaving the page as a themed wrapper.
nodes.step_detail: Aura Atlas of Cradle now asks the solver to map aura traces to locations and follow the solved site chain. Candidate set: Sacred Valley, Blackflame training grounds, Frozen Blade territory, Akura lands, Ghostwater, Night Wheel Valley.

nodes.fairness_scaffold: Named waypoints plus route/zone overlays keep the task about lore and planning rather than blind map memorization.

nodes.title: Correspondence Desk
nodes.surface: The desk is a permanent inbox page with openable threads, a typewriter reply box, and a visible record of earlier help requests.
nodes.mechanics: Each node can register up to three hint letters; when the player has seen the node and owns its prereqs, the desk can issue escalating clues, nudge toward adjacent open fronts, and occasionally surface diegetic reminders about overlooked tools.
nodes.validation: Completion condition is first successful thread unlock after HUB05; afterward it remains a persistent system rather than a one-shot puzzle. Award Reply Protocol on first use.
nodes.build_notes: Represent hints as a table keyed by node_id and hint_level; the UI should never require NLP, only menu-driven requests plus optional flavor text.
nodes.hint_1: You do not solve this page once and leave it forever; it becomes the game's hint interface.
nodes.hint_2: A valid request always targets a visible node or a stuck frontier.
nodes.hint_3: The important reward is the protocol that formalizes how hints are requested.
nodes.cross_talk: Builds an organic hint system into the fiction so help feels like play, not failure.
nodes.step_detail: A simulated inbox and dispatch desk asks the player to thread a few messages correctly; completing it unlocks the in-fiction way to request hints and receive nudges.
nodes.canon_scope: Major mechanic preserved; surrounding content may use more canon flavor but the interaction loop remains mechanics-first.

nodes.title: Torn Dedication
nodes.surface: A separate dedication leaf contains a torn poem, decorative initials, and alt-text on the missing-image placeholders.
nodes.mechanics: Use the Index String to reorder the poem lines, then take an acrostic from the repaired initials and combine it with the placeholder alt-text to extract the Fog Phrase.
nodes.validation: Validation accepts the normalized phrase FOG REMEMBERS DOORS or the correct line order plus alt-text selection state.
nodes.build_notes: Use the same document component as HUB03 but with line reordering and image-hover channels.
nodes.hint_1: Do not read the poem top-to-bottom.
nodes.hint_2: The reordered line initials give only half of the answer.
nodes.hint_3: The image placeholder descriptions finish the phrase.
nodes.cross_talk: Rewards attentive players and reinforces that non-body text can matter.
nodes.step_detail: Read a hidden acrostic formed from image alt text, credits, and decorative drop caps to uncover the phrase that weakens the fog around the unopened realms.

nodes.title: Doors of Assertion
nodes.surface: A hallway of six doors shows compound logical statements, each above a two-lamp input panel for truth assignments.
nodes.mechanics: For each door, toggle p and q so exactly the implication-labeled doors open while the non-equivalent statements remain shut; the open door numbers encode the Lemma of Implication.
nodes.validation: Validation is the exact truth assignment pattern across all doors.
nodes.build_notes: Use a shared proposition parser so later logic nodes can reuse expression rendering.
nodes.hint_1: You are being asked about equivalence classes of statements.
nodes.hint_2: Test what makes an implication false first.
nodes.hint_3: The opened door numbers, in order, form the artifact.
nodes.cross_talk: A friendly formal-logic tutorial that immediately pays off elsewhere.
nodes.step_detail: A corridor of doors is annotated with statements about each door. Determine which worlds are possible and which doors may be open.

nodes.validation: Require the correct canonical mapping or state plus extraction from ordered journal tags.
nodes.build_notes: Timeline cards and solved ordering should be content-only.
nodes.hint_1: Work out event identity before final ordering.
nodes.hint_2: Use the strongest anchor events to fix the spine of the timeline.
nodes.hint_3: Do not extract until the chronology is clean.
nodes.cross_talk: Turns Mother of Learning knowledge into the actual solve material rather than leaving the page as a themed wrapper.
nodes.step_detail: Cyoria Morning Variants now asks the solver to label each morning by loop state and order them by Zorian’s growing knowledge. Candidate set: baseline restart, Zach-aware loop, aranea-aware loop, combat-prep loop, research loop, escape-focused loop.
nodes.canon_scope: the opening month and how repeated mornings diverge across loop states
nodes.fairness_scaffold: A visible event list and snap timeline keep chronology reasoning fair.

nodes.title: Residue Clock
nodes.surface: A large modular clock has hands for mod 3, mod 4, mod 5, and mod 7, each labeling the same hidden integer differently.
nodes.mechanics: Place each event card on the clock time matching its residue conditions; the aligned hand positions around the dial reveal the Mod Wheel token.
nodes.validation: Validation compares all event-card placements.
nodes.build_notes: This should teach the physical intuition of residues, so show all congruence classes visually.
nodes.hint_1: Each card is a statement about the same unknown time.
nodes.hint_2: Start with the tightest modulus.
nodes.hint_3: The pattern of occupied positions around the dial is the artifact.
nodes.cross_talk: A gentle modular arithmetic introduction with strong visual feedback.
nodes.step_detail: A broken clock with several arithmetic residues lets the solver infer a hidden hour sequence modulo different bases.

nodes.title: Madra Well
nodes.surface: The region home page becomes a live Madra Well with current madra, cycling presets, aura intake, and a queue for converting stored madra into charges.
nodes.mechanics: Madra accumulates offline based on core quality, chosen cycling preset, and unlocked aura modifiers. The first milestone is generating 120 total madra and refining three charges; later nodes consume charges but the well always keeps running in the background.
nodes.validation: Mark CRD02 complete once the first three charges are claimed; thereafter it remains interactable and writes continuous state used by later Cradle nodes and a few bosses.
nodes.build_notes: Model this as a deterministic tick system: base_rate * core_multiplier * preset_multiplier * unlocked_modifiers, with coarse 1-minute offline ticks and a capped catch-up window.
nodes.hint_1: You are not waiting for an arbitrary timer; choosing the right cycling preset changes growth.
nodes.hint_2: The first completion threshold is small so the player learns the system quickly.
nodes.hint_3: Keep the well open in another tab if you want later crafting to feel smooth.
nodes.cross_talk: This is the biggest anti-stall toy in the whole ARG: progress continues even when the solver changes fronts.
nodes.step_detail: The player unlocks an idle/incremental madra generator. It keeps producing while other puzzles are solved elsewhere, and later thresholds enable technique crafting and side shortcuts.
nodes.canon_scope: Major mechanic preserved; surrounding content may use more canon flavor but the interaction loop remains mechanics-first.

nodes.validation: Require the correct canonical mapping or state plus extraction from semifinal lane order.
nodes.build_notes: Board state should stay deterministic and low-friction; content supplies zones, roles, and constraints.
nodes.hint_1: Place the irreplaceable roles or anchors first.
nodes.hint_2: A locally sensible move can still violate a larger canon constraint.
nodes.hint_3: Read the final board order only after every piece is legally placed.
nodes.cross_talk: Turns Cradle knowledge into the actual solve material rather than leaving the page as a themed wrapper.
nodes.step_detail: Uncrowned Bracket now asks the solver to rebuild the bracket and assign remembered duel cards. Candidate set: Lindon, Yerin, Mercy, Sophara, Eithan, Ziel, Sha Miara, Yan Shoumei.
nodes.canon_scope: Cradle tournament contenders, factions, and duel memories
nodes.fairness_scaffold: Role tags and explicit board zones keep planning pages crisp and deterministic.

nodes.title: Witnesses and Counterexamples
nodes.surface: A 4x4 quantifier grid lists predicates on rows and small finite domains on columns, with draggable witness and counterexample markers.
nodes.mechanics: For each quantified statement, place either a witness object or a counterexample object in the correct cell; the labels on the successful witness placements spell the Witness Token.
nodes.validation: Validation compares every marker placement against the statement truth values.
nodes.build_notes: This should be a clean teaching puzzle with explicit finite domains shown on screen.
nodes.hint_1: Translate each sentence into 'for all' or 'there exists' before touching the grid.
nodes.hint_2: A single counterexample is enough to kill a universal statement.
nodes.hint_3: Only the existentially true statements contribute letters.
nodes.cross_talk: Makes abstract logic concrete and creates a reusable “find the counterexample” habit.
nodes.step_detail: Choose the smallest set of examples that make a collection of universal and existential statements true or false.

nodes.title: Fitch Staircase
nodes.surface: A staircase of proof lines appears with draggable statements, assumption bars, and inference badges.
nodes.mechanics: Assemble a valid natural-deduction proof for a target sequent using introduction and elimination rules; the line numbers on the essential assumptions yield the Proof Frame.
nodes.validation: Validation is a full proof-state check including line dependencies, not just final conclusion matching.
nodes.build_notes: Build a minimal proof-checker that validates scope and cited lines so the component can power other proof puzzles later.
nodes.hint_1: Start from the target connective and ask what rule could introduce it.
nodes.hint_2: Scope bars matter; some candidate steps are locally valid but globally illegal.
nodes.hint_3: The artifact comes from the assumptions that survive into the final proof skeleton.
nodes.cross_talk: Introduces proof structure as a puzzle mechanic instead of a test.
nodes.step_detail: Arrange proof steps in a staircase of assumptions and discharge lines to complete a compact natural deduction proof.

nodes.title: Prime Factor Locksmith
nodes.surface: A locksmith bench shows five brass locks, each opening only when the supplied number has the required prime-factor profile.
nodes.mechanics: Build factor trees for the candidate numbers, place each number into its compatible lock, and read the prime exponents from the opened teeth to obtain Prime Teeth.
nodes.validation: Validation is the number-to-lock assignment.
nodes.build_notes: Factor trees can be auto-generated as the user drags primes, but final placement should still be intentional.
nodes.hint_1: The locks care about factor shape, not just magnitude.
nodes.hint_2: Two numbers share a prime but differ in exponent, which matters.
nodes.hint_3: Read exponents from the lock teeth left to right.
nodes.cross_talk: Makes factorization tactile and memorable.
nodes.step_detail: Several locks open only when their labels are decomposed into the right prime factors and recombined in a particular order.

nodes.title: Chinese Gate
nodes.surface: A gate console presents three incomplete congruence systems and a set of candidate remainders.
nodes.mechanics: Reconstruct the one integer for each system that satisfies all displayed residues, then compare the solutions modulo a larger base; the paired surviving residues form the Congruence Pair.
nodes.validation: Validation checks each reconstructed solution class.
nodes.build_notes: Keep the arithmetic small enough to be solved with reasoning rather than grinding.
nodes.hint_1: Solve each system by combining moduli step by step.
nodes.hint_2: The larger base comparison happens after you solve the three systems.
nodes.hint_3: Only the paired residues that agree under the larger base become the artifact.
nodes.cross_talk: Establishes CRT as a reusable solver tool for later sections.
nodes.step_detail: Fragments of a gate inscription give several residues and moduli; reconstruct the smallest valid gate number.

nodes.hint_2: Use category or compatibility constraints before fine distinctions.

nodes.title: Delivery Board
nodes.surface: The inn home page gains a live delivery board with order cards, staff tokens, time windows, route costs, and optional door-peg shortcuts.
nodes.mechanics: The first milestone is successfully schedule and complete four deliveries over one in-game day without missed windows or spoiled hot food. Later puzzles can add new order cards or constraints, but the board stays persistent as a logistics toy.
nodes.validation: CRD-style completion is milestone based: when the first perfect day resolves, award Hospitality Token and keep the board active for future runs.
nodes.build_notes: Model this as turnless scheduling: resolve one in-game day when the user hits Dispatch, with deterministic travel-time simulation and saved unlocked staff traits.
nodes.hint_1: The puzzle is to assign staff and sequence, not to click quickly.
nodes.hint_2: One delivery is only possible if you reserve a fast runner for the late slot.
nodes.hint_3: Perfect completion means no missed windows and no spoiled meals.
nodes.cross_talk: Adds a persistent low-pressure management toy so the region is not just one-shot puzzles.
nodes.step_detail: A standing inn-management board asks the player to place deliveries, guests, and staff efficiently. Correct weekly layouts unlock a hospitality token and later side clues.

nodes.title: The Marginalia Return
nodes.surface: The whole site subtly changes after the Continuity Flame: old pages gain faint marginal letters, hidden layers, and new clickable seams.

nodes.hint_3: The verified ordered letters are the reward.

nodes.step_detail: Use the Door Sigil, world-hop structure, congruence timing, and curvature navigation to chart the only valid path through the twelve final doors.

nodes.title: The Redacted Author

nodes.mechanics: Use the Palimpsest Letters to unmask the correct redaction channels, then combine crisis logic, storyweight law, and desk correspondence patterns to determine what the hidden authorial motive must have been. The resulting motive phrase is the Author Fragment.

nodes.build_notes: This should read like a mystery about intent, not a technical file audit.
nodes.hint_1: Different channels reveal different kinds of truth: literal, narrative, and procedural.
nodes.hint_2: The desk protocol tells you which comments are authentic.
nodes.hint_3: The motive phrase is the reward.
nodes.cross_talk: Lets the website itself become the final mystery object.
nodes.step_detail: A final layer of comments, redactions, and archival cross-reference reveals the hidden authorial voice inside the nexus machinery.

nodes.title: The Worldline Proof
nodes.surface: A final theorem room combines the door order, hidden motive, combined build, adjudicated ruling, and worldline overlay into one last metameta.
nodes.mechanics: Use the Door Sequence as the statement order, the Author Fragment as the intended interpretation, the Seal of Ascent and Judge's Mandate as side constraints, and the Worldline Prism as the model. The only coherent final proof yields the True Name / Final Answer.
nodes.validation: Validation is a full structured meta-state and the final normalized answer string.

nodes.hint_2: The author fragment tells you what the ambiguous symbols mean.
nodes.hint_3: When everything is mapped correctly, the final answer should feel inevitable.
nodes.cross_talk: This should be the single biggest aha in the entire experience.
nodes.step_detail: Everything converges: route, author, progression, catastrophe, and worldline. The player assembles the definitive proof-story-name that resolves the ARG.

systems.name: Global Shell and Inventory Layer
systems.ui: Persistent header with current section, artifact tray, inbox badge, progress rings, and accessibility menu. Mobile view collapses map and inventory into drawers.
systems.state: player_state, unlocked_nodes, solved_nodes, artifact_inventory, hint_status, section_progress, recent_events.
systems.logic: On every route load, query unlocked nodes and artifacts, render visible affordances, and precompute adjacent frontier suggestions.
systems.integration: Every node writes to the same inventory/unlock engine. Region hubs subscribe to unlock changes and re-render instantly.

systems.name: Correspondence Desk
systems.ui: Thread list, open thread view, request controls (nudge / stronger nudge / extraction check), archival notes, and follow-up suggestions tied to open frontiers.
systems.logic: The desk is menu-driven. A request always targets a visible node or frontier. The system checks visibility + prereqs, increments hint level, writes request history, and returns the authored response.
systems.integration: Several document and final-arc puzzles inspect desk metadata; therefore every hint should have a stable letter id and timestamp stored in content.

systems.name: Madra Well
systems.ui: Current madra, growth rate, aura channels, cycling presets, conversion queue, equipped techniques, craft hooks, and milestone tracker.
systems.state: madra_pool, total_generated, core_quality, aura_channels, preset_id, unlocked_presets, technique_nodes, queued_conversions, charge_count, last_tick_at.
systems.logic: Offline growth = elapsed_minutes * base_rate * core_multiplier * preset_multiplier * product(unlocked modifiers), with a catch-up cap. Claiming charges consumes pool; later nodes read both charges and equipped techniques.
systems.integration: CRD05, CRD06, CRD11, DCC11, and CV05 read well state. The region hub should expose a compact live widget so the player can leave and return naturally.

systems.name: Delivery Board
systems.ui: Order cards, staff tokens, time windows, travel map summary, spoilage indicators, dispatch button, and previous-day log.
systems.state: available_staff, staff_traits, pending_orders, completed_orders, door_shortcuts, current_day_seed, delivery_runs, perfect_days.
systems.logic: Dispatch resolves a deterministic schedule against travel times, staff speed, order constraints, and optional door shortcuts. Outcomes write completed runs and unlock later clue surfaces.
systems.integration: TWI08 and TWI09 check delivery state. Later bosses can import staff traits or successful route history without reopening the scheduler logic.

systems.name: Dungeon Crawl
systems.ui: Room graph, current room panel, visible inventory, health/stamina bars, room log, discovered patrols, and interaction buttons.
systems.state: room_states, room_flags, inventory, equipped_items, keyring, crawl_position, known_patrols, scripted_events, tutorial_cleared.
systems.logic: Every room is a typed script (trap, loot, door, encounter, puzzle, safe room). Player actions are deterministic commands that mutate room and inventory state. No twitch timing is required; even trap rooms resolve against scripted cycles.
systems.integration: Later DCC nodes can add room overlays, keys, patrol knowledge, or contract conditions without replacing the system.

systems.name: Page Mutation / Revisit Layer
systems.routes: Used by MOL06, TWI09, AA08, FIN01, and any later revisit content
systems.ui: No dedicated page. Instead, mutated routes expose alt versions, extra marginalia, hidden hotspots, or changed metadata once the relevant flags are set.
systems.logic: A route resolves its variant by consulting mutation flags. Content authors mark which DOM fragments, annotations, or assets change at each mutation level.
systems.integration: This layer is essential for long-form delight because it makes old knowledge newly useful without rewriting pages by hand.

Hub.summary: The nexus is the site shell. It teaches interaction grammar, establishes the archive fiction, and opens the first and second content waves.
Hub.layout: Landing page uses a central canvas with marginalia and orbit controls. HUB06 lives as a permanent desk route. Once HUB08 falls, the nexus map expands and adds second-wave region sockets.
Hub.notes: This area must be friction-light. It teaches what counts as a clue source, what counts as a stateful tool, and where the player asks for help.

Cradle.summary: Cradle now mixes progression systems with canon-derived Path, aura, technique, tournament, and revelation pages.
Cradle.layout: Region hub foregrounds the Madra Well widget, then links out to technique, crafting, anatomy, and late trial pages. The boss imports build state rather than acting like a standalone riddle.
Cradle.notes: Keep the Madra Well and crafting/build tools intact, but make feeder pages depend on real sacred-arts familiarity.

Wandering Inn.summary: The inn region is social-logistical. It uses ledgers, schedules, routes, chess, room logic, and one persistent delivery board.
Wandering Inn.layout: Hub shows inn board summary, guest count, and route map. Puzzle pages are mostly board and map templates; TWI05 is the living system page.
Wandering Inn.notes: The emotional feel is hospitality under pressure. Use warm visual design and clear consequences when logistics fail.

Worm.summary: Worm now leans on actual capes, triggers, classifications, factions, incidents, and tinker specialties.
Worm.layout: Hub acts like a case room with access to map, evidence, lab, and signal pages. Later nodes should visibly import earlier threat models.
Worm.notes: Keep the case-room shell, but stop teaching the world from zero on every page; reward existing knowledge.

MoL.summary: Mother of Learning is the revisit-and-memory region. It specializes in diffing, loops, calendars, state retention, and controlled revisitation.
MoL.layout: Hub is a loop journal with entries unlocking from resets. Several nodes mutate earlier pages or version histories rather than staying fully local.
MoL.notes: Make revisits discoverable. Never require random page rechecking without a manifest, loop log, or hint-desk nudge.

Logic.summary: Hall of Proofs is the formal-language overlay. It teaches truth conditions, witness logic, proof assembly, modal reachability, and consistency.
Logic.layout: A clean corridor-like hub opens one proof room at a time, then collects seals into the final theorem door.
Logic.notes: This section exists partly to reduce arbitrary ARG leaps. It teaches the player what a valid formal step looks like.

Number Theory.summary: Prime Vault is the modular arithmetic overlay. It provides clocks, factors, CRT, residues, and a cipher payoff.
Number Theory.layout: Vault doors, dials, and instruments dominate the UI. The section should visually model modular ideas instead of leaving them as text-only math.
Number Theory.notes: Small numbers, strong visuals, and deterministic helpers keep the section fair while still feeling collegiate.

Arcane Ascension.summary: Arcane Ascension now uses attunements, Judgments, rune functions, archive provenance, creatures, cast knowledge, and duel toolkits.
Arcane Ascension.layout: Hub resembles a spire interior. The hub surface can display current attunement, accessible floors, and crafted items.
Arcane Ascension.notes: Preserve crafting, but make the surrounding pages canon-facing and Kaldwyn-specific.

Abstract Algebra.summary: Symmetry Forge turns finite algebra into tactile structure matching: Cayley tables, permutation action, isomorphism, lattices, and homomorphisms.
Abstract Algebra.layout: A workshop presentation is friendlier than blackboard abstraction. The boss is an action-on-set visual rather than a proof essay.
Abstract Algebra.notes: Favor finite examples with manipulable structure. The section should reward pattern recognition and invariants.

Cosmere.summary: Cosmere is the worldhop and layered-reality region. It uses world maps, realm overlays, spectrum sorting, ledgers, geometric figures, and correspondence archives.
Cosmere.layout: Hub acts like a star map with realm filters. Several nodes share overlay and route components, then culminate in a fracture reconstruction boss.
Cosmere.notes: This region is a good home for visually rich puzzles and one explicitly web-native page.

Dungeon Crawler Carl.summary: DCC now uses canon items, achievements, floor gimmicks, contracts, audience reactions, and floor geography while keeping the crawl toys intact.
Dungeon Crawler Carl.layout: Hub is the live crawl with openings to item, sponsor, and borough subpages. Later node rewards can patch new states directly into the crawl.
Dungeon Crawler Carl.notes: Preserve crawl and crafting systems, but let the surrounding feeders feel like remembered crawl television.

Differential Geometry.summary: Curved Atlas is the geometric overlay. It delivers geodesics, chart transitions, curvature, transport, and vector fields through pictures and manipulable surfaces.
Differential Geometry.layout: A map-room or gallery presentation works best. Every page should foreground diagrams and transformation toggles.
Differential Geometry.notes: Keep the concepts visual and qualitative wherever possible. This section should feel surprisingly approachable.

Practical Guide.summary: This region is narrative-law and role pressure: choice invariants, Names, formations, deontics, contracts, verse, claims, and knot logic.
Practical Guide.layout: Hub feels like a court-camp hybrid. Many pages are board or document puzzles, but the throughline is always narrative force rather than raw lore.
Practical Guide.notes: This section becomes a bridge between literary theming and formal logic. Make the rules explicit and legible.

Crossovers.summary: The crossover layer turns prior outputs into persistent tools and then uses those tools in late bosses.
Crossovers.layout: Each tool-builder meta is a compact, elegant page; each convergence boss is a synthesis chamber that imports artifacts and system states.
Crossovers.notes: The crossovers are where the whole ARG starts to feel like one machine rather than many rooms.

Final Arc.summary: The final arc revisits the entire archive, mutates earlier pages, and converts the whole graph into one final proof-shaped supermeta.
Final Arc.layout: Use archive and vault imagery, but keep the information architecture crisp. Final pages should feel cumulative, not cluttered.
Final Arc.notes: Everything here depends on explicit mapping of old artifacts to new roles; avoid any final leap that has no visible bridge.
